data.title: Is InMode Ltd.'s (NASDAQ:INMD) Recent Stock Performance Tethered To Its Strong Fundamentals?
data.description:
InMode (NASDAQ:INMD) has had a great run on the share market with its stock up by a significant 10% over the last week. Since the market usually pay for a company’s long-term fundamentals, we decided to study the company’s key performance indicators to see if they could be influencing the market. Particularly, we will be paying attention to InMode's ROE today.

Return on equity or ROE is a key measure used to assess how efficiently a company's management is utilizing the company's capital. In short, ROE shows the profit each dollar generates with respect to its shareholder investments.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

How To Calculate Return On Equity?

ROE can be calculated by using the formula:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for InMode is:

23% = US$150m ÷ US$653m (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

The 'return' refers to a company's earnings over the last year. Another way to think of that is that for every $1 worth of equity, the company was able to earn $0.23 in profit.

See our latest analysis for InMode

What Is The Relationship Between ROE And Earnings Growth?

So far, we've learned that ROE is a measure of a company's profitability. Based on how much of its profits the company chooses to reinvest or "retain", we are then able to evaluate a company's future ability to generate profits. Assuming everything else remains unchanged, the higher the ROE and profit retention, the higher the growth rate of a company compared to companies that don't necessarily bear these characteristics.

InMode's Earnings Growth And 23% ROE

To start with, InMode's ROE looks acceptable. Further, the company's ROE compares quite favorably to the industry average of 9.8%. This certainly adds some context to InMode's decent 10.0% net income growth seen over the past five years.

As a next step, we compared InMode's net income growth with the industry and found that the company has a similar growth figure when compared with the industry average growth rate of 12% in the same period.

Earnings growth is a huge factor in stock valuation. The investor should try to establish if the expected growth or decline in earnings, whichever the case may be, is priced in. By doing so, they will have an idea if the stock is headed into clear blue waters or if swampy waters await. One good indicator of expected earnings growth is the P/E ratio which determines the price the market is willing to pay for a stock based on its earnings prospects. So, you may want to check if InMode is trading on a high P/E or a low P/E, relative to its industry.

Is InMode Efficiently Re-investing Its Profits?

InMode doesn't pay any regular dividends, meaning that all of its profits are being reinvested in the business, which explains the fair bit of earnings growth the company has seen.

Summary

Overall, we are quite pleased with InMode's performance. Particularly, we like that the company is reinvesting heavily into its business, and at a high rate of return. Unsurprisingly, this has led to an impressive earnings growth. That being so, according to the latest industry analyst forecasts, the company's earnings are expected to shrink in the future. • Over the past week, International Business Machines (NYSE:IBM) joined more than 100 organizations in supporting Open Invention Network's expanded OIN 2.0 patent protection and funding model for open source software.
  • IBM announced new agentic AI collaborations with global partners, including e&, focused on real world use cases such as compliance and operational efficiency.
  • The company also launched AI driven digital experiences for the GRAMMYs and introduced Enterprise Advantage, a generative AI consulting service for clients building internal AI platforms.

For investors watching enterprise technology, these moves offer a snapshot of how IBM is positioning itself across open source, AI software, and consulting services. The company operates at the intersection of infrastructure, software, and services, and these updates highlight IBM's focus on AI tools designed for regulated and large scale environments.

Open source risk management, enterprise grade generative AI, and industry specific digital experiences are themes many large corporates are working through right now. IBM's latest announcements provide additional data points on how NYSE:IBM is competing for those budgets and partnerships, and how its role in ecosystems like OIN and high profile events like the GRAMMYs may influence client engagement over time.

data.title: Is Chewy (CHWY) Pricing Reflect Recent Share Weakness And DCF Versus P/E Signals
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Chewy's current share price lines up with its true value, this article is designed to walk you through that question step by step.
  • Chewy's shares last closed at US$31.30, with returns of a 5.1% decline over 7 days, a 3.7% decline over 30 days, a 6.5% decline year to date, a 19.5% decline over 1 year, a 30.5% decline over 3 years, and a 69.2% decline over 5 years. These figures may affect how investors are thinking about both risk and potential upside.
  • Recent headlines around Chewy have focused on how the business is positioning itself within the online pet retail space, and how its customer relationships and subscription style offerings fit into that picture. This backdrop provides useful context when you weigh up the share price moves against the underlying business story.
  • On our checks, Chewy scores 3 out of 6 on valuation, as shown by its value score of 3. We will walk through what different valuation methods say about that number and finish with a way to look at valuation that can give you a fuller picture than any single metric alone.

Find out why Chewy's -19.5% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Chewy Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model takes estimates of the cash a business could generate in the future and discounts those amounts back to today. This provides a single estimate of what the equity might be worth per share right now.

For Chewy, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $476.8 million. Analysts, together with Simply Wall St extrapolations beyond the analyst horizon, project annual free cash flow reaching $1,240.0 million by 2030, with further projections out to 2035. All of these cash flows are in US$, then discounted back to today using the DCF framework.

Putting those projections together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $56.48 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $31.30, this model suggests the stock screens as around 44.6% undervalued.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Chewy is undervalued by 44.6%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 876 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Chewy.

Approach 2: Chewy Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It lets you quickly compare how the market is pricing those earnings relative to other businesses.

What counts as a "normal" P/E depends a lot on two things: growth expectations and risk. Higher expected earnings growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually lines up with a lower one.

Chewy currently trades on a P/E of 62.92x. That sits above the Specialty Retail industry average of 20.05x and the peer group average of 25.65x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary Fair Ratio of 28.50x, which is the P/E it would expect for Chewy given factors like its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio can be more useful than a simple peer or industry comparison because it aims to adjust for differences in growth, risk, profitability, size and sector, rather than assuming all retailers should trade on the same multiple. Comparing Chewy's current P/E of 62.92x with the Fair Ratio of 28.50x suggests the shares screen as overvalued on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

A Narrative is simply your view of the business written into a forecast, then linked directly to an estimated fair value per share. This way, you are not just looking at ratios in isolation, but at how Chewy’s business story could play out in its financials.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, used by millions of investors, you can browse and create these Narratives, compare fair values with the current Chewy share price, and use that gap to help you decide whether you see conditions as closer to a buy, a hold, or a sell.
